title: Update Dockerfile to Node.js base image
description:
Replace the Bun base image with a Node.js Alpine image and update all install/build/run commands to use pnpm and node.

## Current Dockerfile (26 lines)
- Base image: docker.io/oven/bun:1-alpine
- Install: bun install --frozen-lockfile
- Build: bun run build
- Run: bun run .output/server
- Lock file copied: bun.lock*

## Required changes

Stage: base
- Change FROM line to node:24-alpine
- Add pnpm setup: RUN corepack enable && corepack prepare pnpm@10.24.0 --activate

Stage: deps
- Keep apk add python3 make g++ (still needed for better-sqlite3 native compilation)
- Change COPY package.json bun.lock* to COPY package.json pnpm-lock.yaml
- Add COPY .npmrc ./ if .npmrc is added by CE-g3m
- Change RUN bun install --frozen-lockfile to RUN pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

Stage: build
- Change RUN bun run build to RUN pnpm run build

Stage: runtime
- Change CMD bun run .output/server to CMD node .output/server

## Notes
- better-sqlite3 is a native addon that requires python3/make/g++ for compilation, keep those apk deps
- The runtime stage does not need pnpm/node_modules; only .output/ is copied, so the CMD runs with plain node
- Nitro node-server preset produces a self-contained .output/server/index.mjs

## Acceptance criteria
- docker build completes successfully
- Container starts and serves the app on port 3000
- No bun references remain in Dockerfile
close_reason: Closed

title: Update Dockerfile to Node.js base image
description:
Replace the Bun base image with a Node.js Alpine image and update all install/build/run commands to use pnpm and node.

## Current Dockerfile (26 lines)
- Base image: `docker.io/oven/bun:1-alpine`
- Install: `bun install --frozen-lockfile`
- Build: `bun run build`
- Run: `bun run .output/server`
- Lock file copied: `bun.lock*`

## Required changes

**Stage: base**
- Change FROM line to `node:24-alpine`
- Add pnpm setup: `RUN corepack enable && corepack prepare pnpm@10.24.0 --activate`

**Stage: deps**
- Keep `apk add python3 make g++` (still needed for better-sqlite3 native compilation)
- Change `COPY package.json bun.lock* ./` → `COPY package.json pnpm-lock.yaml ./`
- Add `COPY .npmrc ./` (if .npmrc is added by CE-g3m)
- Change `RUN bun install --frozen-lockfile` → `RUN pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`

**Stage: build**
- Change `RUN bun run build` → `RUN pnpm run build`

**Stage: runtime**
- Change `CMD ["bun", "run", ".output/server"]` → `CMD ["node", ".output/server"]`

## Notes
- `better-sqlite3` is a native addon that requires python3/make/g++ for compilation — keep those apk deps
- The runtime stage should NOT have pnpm/node_modules; only .output/ is copied, so the CMD runs with plain `node`
- Nitro `node-server` preset produces a self-contained .output/server/index.mjs — `node .output/server` should work directly (verify after CE-26t is done)

## Acceptance criteria
- `docker build -t camera-events-v2 .` completes successfully
- Container starts and serves the app on :3000
- No bun references remain in Dockerfile
close_reason: Duplicate of CE-31q (created twice in parallel)

title: Update Nitro preset from bun to node-server
description:
Change the Nitro server preset in vite.config.ts from 'bun' to 'node-server' so the production build targets Node.js instead of Bun's runtime.

## File to change
`vite.config.ts` line 67

## Current
```ts
nitro({ preset: 'bun' })
```

## Change to
```ts
nitro({ preset: 'node-server' })
```

## Why node-server not node
The 'node' preset produces a minimal entry without an HTTP listener — it expects the caller to wire up the server. The 'node-server' preset bundles Nitro's built-in HTTP server and is the standard preset for Node.js deployments. TanStack Start's server handler integrates via the HTTP listener that 'node-server' provides.

## Verification
After changing the preset:
1. Run `pnpm run build`
2. Run `node .output/server`
3. App must respond on :3000

## Acceptance criteria
- Build succeeds with node-server preset
- Production server starts with plain `node .output/server`
- No Bun-specific output artifacts in .output/
close_reason: Closed

title: Migrate package manager from bun to pnpm
description:
Switch the project from Bun as package manager to pnpm + Node.js (v24). This is the foundational change that unlocks all downstream migration tasks.

## What needs to change

**package.json:**
- Change `"preinstall": "npx only-allow bun"` → `"npx only-allow pnpm"`
- Change `"start": "bun run .output/server"` → `"node .output/server"`
- Remove `"verify:bun-bundle"` script (Bun-specific guard, no longer relevant)
- Add `"packageManager": "pnpm@10.24.0"` field
- Add `"engines": { "node": ">=24", "pnpm": ">=10" }` field
- Move `better-sqlite3` from `devDependencies` to `dependencies` (it will be needed at runtime once the Bun branch is removed)

**Lock file:**
- Delete `bun.lock`
- Run `pnpm install` to generate `pnpm-lock.yaml`

**pnpm config:**
- Add `.npmrc` with `shamefully-hoist=true` (or use `node-linker=hoisted`) to ensure TanStack Start and Nitro can resolve their deep dependencies. Without hoisting, TanStack Start's server bundles may fail to resolve native ESM packages.

**Node version pin:**
- Add `.nvmrc` containing `24` (matches local Node v24.9.0)

## Acceptance criteria
- `pnpm install` completes without errors
- `pnpm run dev` starts dev server on :3000
- `pnpm run build` completes successfully
- `pnpm run test` passes
- No `bun` references remain in package.json scripts
close_reason: Closed

title: Remove vite auxiliary files: vite-plugin-sw.ts and verify-bun-bundle.mjs
description:
Two auxiliary files alongside vite.config.ts were added as workarounds or Bun-specific guards. These should be removed or replaced as part of the Node.js migration.

## vite-plugin-sw.ts
This 80-line custom Vite plugin builds the service worker (src/sw.ts) in two phases: it bundles sw.ts with Vite, then uses @serwist/build to inject the precache manifest in production. It was written as a workaround because vite-plugin-pwa does not support TanStack Start's Vite 6 Environment API.

**Action**: Investigate whether vite-plugin-pwa now supports the Vite 6 Environment API (check the vite-plugin-pwa changelog and latest release). If it does, replace the custom plugin with vite-plugin-pwa and the standard Serwist integration. If it still does not, keep vite-plugin-sw.ts in place — it is not Bun-specific and the workaround remains valid. In either case, document the decision in a comment.

## scripts/verify-bun-bundle.mjs
This 72-line script scans .output/server .mjs files and fails if better-sqlite3 appears as a static import (it was needed to ensure better-sqlite3 was not bundled into the Bun production image). With the switch to Node, better-sqlite3 is always the runtime driver and there is no longer anything to guard against.

**Action**: Delete scripts/verify-bun-bundle.mjs. Remove the 'verify:bun-bundle' script from package.json if not already done by CE-g3m.

## Inline vite.config.ts cleanup
While not a separate file, review whether the tanstackStartTestStubs() inline plugin and the optimizeDeps.exclude block are still needed after removing the Bun preset. These were added in recent commits to work around TanStack Start virtual module resolution in test mode. If they remain necessary, add a comment explaining why. If they can be removed, do so.

## Acceptance criteria
- scripts/verify-bun-bundle.mjs is deleted
- No 'verify:bun-bundle' script in package.json
- vite-plugin-sw.ts is either removed (if vite-plugin-pwa works) or has a clear comment explaining why it is kept
- pnpm run build and pnpm run test pass
close_reason: Closed

title: Simplify SQLite driver: remove Bun branch, make better-sqlite3 the only driver
description:
The file src/features/shared/server/sqlite/index.ts currently has a runtime branch: it uses bun:sqlite when process.versions.bun is set, and better-sqlite3 on Node. Since we are migrating to Node-only, the Bun branch can be removed entirely and the abstraction simplified.

Current architecture is 170 lines with: isBunRuntime() check, Node branch wrapping better-sqlite3 in a promise (lines 51-80), Bun branch with lazy dynamic import of bun:sqlite and type stubs (lines 82-169). openSqlite() is async because the Bun branch needs dynamic import.

Target: Remove isBunRuntime(), loadBunSqlite(), BunSqliteDatabase/BunSqliteStatement type stubs, and all Bun code. openSqlite(path) can remain async to avoid changing callers. The SqliteDatabase interface (prepare, exec, pragmaRead, pragmaWrite, close) stays unchanged. Remove the @vite-ignore comment.

Dependency change: better-sqlite3 must move from devDependencies to dependencies since it is now the production driver. Coordinate with CE-g3m.

Acceptance criteria: index.ts no longer references bun, bun:sqlite, or process.versions.bun. pnpm run test passes. better-sqlite3 is in dependencies.
close_reason: Closed

title: Simplify SQLite driver: remove Bun branch, make better-sqlite3 the only driver
description:
The file src/features/shared/server/sqlite/index.ts currently has a runtime branch: it uses bun:sqlite when process.versions.bun is set, and better-sqlite3 on Node. Since we are migrating to Node-only, the Bun branch can be removed entirely and the abstraction simplified.

## Current architecture (170 lines)
- isBunRuntime() checks process.versions.bun (lines 32-36)
- Node branch (lines 51-80): wraps better-sqlite3 in a promise, matches SqliteDatabase interface
- Bun branch (lines 82-169): lazy dynamic import of bun:sqlite, type stubs for BunSqliteDatabase/BunSqliteStatement
- openSqlite() is async because the Bun branch needs dynamic import

## Target architecture
Since we always run on Node:
- Remove isBunRuntime(), loadBunSqlite(), BunSqliteDatabase/BunSqliteStatement type stubs, and the entire Bun code path
- openSqlite(path) can remain async (or become sync) — prefer keeping it async to avoid changing callers
- Remove the @vite-ignore comment (no longer needed)
- The SqliteDatabase interface stays unchanged (callers already use it)

## What to keep
- The SqliteDatabase interface (prepare, exec, pragmaRead, pragmaWrite, close) — callers depend on it
- The Node branch wrapping logic (already correct)
- better-sqlite3 as the implementation

## What to remove
- Everything from line 82 to end (Bun branch)
- isBunRuntime() function
- The conditional logic that picks branches

## Dependency change
better-sqlite3 must be in dependencies (not devDependencies) since it is now the production runtime driver. This change should be coordinated with CE-g3m (package.json migration).

## Acceptance criteria
- src/features/shared/server/sqlite/index.ts no longer references bun, bun:sqlite, or process.versions.bun
- pnpm run test passes (all sqlite tests pass)
- better-sqlite3 is listed in dependencies in package.json
close_reason: Duplicate of CE-tj5 (created twice in parallel)

title: Remove Bun-specific SQLite test files
description:
After the SQLite driver is simplified to Node-only (CE-1yk), two test files that exist solely to test the Bun code path should be deleted.

## Files to delete
1. src/features/shared/server/sqlite/sqlite.bun-runtime.test.ts (84 lines)
   - Skips itself on non-Bun runtimes via describe.skipIf(!isBun)
   - Tests the bun:sqlite production code path with a real Bun runtime
   - No longer relevant once the Bun branch is removed

2. src/features/shared/server/sqlite/sqlite.bun-branch.test.ts (173 lines)
   - Mocks process.versions.bun and bun:sqlite to test the Bun code path on Node
   - Tests branch selection, statement delegation, exec→db.run mapping, PRAGMA handling
   - All the behavior it tests will no longer exist after CE-1yk

## Files to review
- src/features/shared/server/sqlite/sqlite.test.ts — main driver test, uses better-sqlite3 on Node. Review for any comments or setup that references Bun, and remove stale references.
- src/features/push-notifications/server/push-store.driver-contract.test.ts — driver contract test, mentions SQLite driver swap in comments. Review and clean up stale references.
- src/features/settings/hooks/usePushSubscription.test.ts — mocks an error message about better-sqlite3 not being supported in Bun (line 16), and asserts the error does not contain 'Bun' (line 22). Review whether this test logic still makes sense once Bun is gone.

## Acceptance criteria
- sqlite.bun-runtime.test.ts and sqlite.bun-branch.test.ts are deleted
- pnpm run test passes with zero failures
- No test file contains stale references to Bun runtime selection
close_reason: Closed

title: Add Favorites nav link to Header
description:
Add a Favorites entry to the top navigation bar so users can reach /favorites from any page.

In src/features/shell/components/Header.tsx, modify the getHeaderAuthState() navLinks array (currently at lines 25-31) to add:
  { label: 'Favorites', to: '/favorites' }

Position it between Events and Settings so the order becomes: Cameras → Events → Favorites → Settings.

Update any Header stories or snapshot tests that assert the exact list of nav items. The nav link will receive the same is-active class treatment as existing links (TanStack Router's activeProps already handles this).
close_reason: Closed

title: Create /favorites authenticated route
description:
Add src/routes/_authenticated/favorites.tsx to create the /favorites route, following the same structure as camera-events.index.tsx.

Loader:
- Call getFavoriteEventsFn() and getFavoritedEventIdsFn() in parallel (Promise.all or TanStack loader pattern)
- Return { events: FrigateEvent[], favoriteEventIds: string[] }

Page component:
- Wrap with Suspense using the CameraEventsLoading skeleton as fallback
- Render FavoritesListPage with loader data
- Add pull-to-refresh support using the same usePullToRefresh pattern as camera-events.index.tsx (threshold 80px)
- Call useRefetchOnMount() and useRefetchOnFocus() so the list stays current when navigating back

The route file does NOT regenerate automatically — after adding this file, start the dev server (bun run dev) to trigger routeTree.gen.ts regeneration. Note this in the implementation step.
close_reason: Closed

title: Create FavoritesListPage component and stories
description:
A new page component that displays a user's favorited events in the same EventCard grid used by the events list page.

EventCard is currently defined inline inside CameraEventsListPage. Before writing FavoritesListPage, extract it to src/features/camera-events/components/EventCard.tsx so both pages can import it within the same feature slice without duplication.

FavoritesListPage props:
  events: FrigateEvent[]
  favoriteEventIds: string[]

Behaviour:
- Renders the same EventCard grid as CameraEventsListPage (same card layout, FavoriteButton overlay, link to /camera-events/:id detail page)
- Each card passes isFavorited={favoriteIdSet.has(event.id)} — the set lets the FavoriteButton reflect optimistic state correctly even if the user unfavorites while on this page
- Empty state: show a centred message 'No favorited events yet. Tap the heart on any event to save it here.' — same visual treatment as the empty state in CameraEventsListPage
- Loading state: reuse the CameraEventsLoading skeleton exported from CameraEventsListPage
- No filter controls needed — the list is already scoped to the user's favorites

Stories (FavoritesListPage.stories.tsx):
- WithEvents story: 6 mock FrigateEvent fixtures, all favorited
- Empty story: events=[], favoriteEventIds=[]
- Loading story: uses CameraEventsLoading

Tests (FavoritesListPage.test.tsx):
- Renders event cards for each event
- Empty state renders when events=[]
- FavoriteButton receives isFavorited=true for events in favoriteEventIds
- Each card links to the correct detail URL
close_reason: Closed

title: Add getFavoriteEventsFn server function
description:
The favorites store only persists event IDs. The Frigate API has no bulk-fetch-by-IDs endpoint, so we need a server function that resolves full FrigateEvent objects for all of a user's favorites.

Add getFavoriteEventsFn (GET) to src/features/camera-events/server/favorites.ts:
1. requireSession() → userId
2. getFavoritedEventIdsFn / store.getFavoritedEventIds(userId) → string[]
3. If empty, return []
4. Promise.all(ids.map(id => getEvent(id))) in parallel
5. Collect only { ok: true } results; silently drop events that Frigate can no longer find (deleted/expired)
6. Return FrigateEvent[]

Export a handleGetFavoriteEvents(userId, store, frigateClient) pure helper alongside it (same testability pattern as handleToggleFavorite uses for handleGetFavoritedEventIds). The route loader will call the server function; tests call the pure helper directly.

TDD order:
- RED: test empty favorites returns [], test n favorites fetches n events in parallel, test missing events are omitted from results, test requireSession is called
- GREEN: implement
- REFACTOR: clean up

Add tests in favorites.test.ts.
close_reason: Closed

title: Wire Frigate retain sync into handleToggleFavorite
description:
handleToggleFavorite (src/features/camera-events/server/favorites.ts) currently only toggles the SQLite row. It must also sync the retain_indefinitely flag on the Frigate API so that favorited events are never auto-deleted by Frigate.

Business rules:
- When toggling ON (was unfavorited → now favorited): call retainEvent(eventId) on Frigate. This is the first-favorite case — any other existing favorites mean Frigate is already retained, but calling retain again is idempotent.
- When toggling OFF (was favorited → now unfavorited): call getFavoriteCount(eventId) after removeFavorite. If count === 0, call unretainEvent(eventId) — this was the last user, so Frigate can resume normal retention rules.

Error handling: Frigate errors must not block the favorite toggle. Log the error but return { ok: true } with the updated isFavorited value regardless. The SQLite state is authoritative for the UI; Frigate retention is best-effort.

The signature of handleToggleFavorite needs a frigateClient parameter (or similar injectable) for testability — do not import the client module directly in the business-logic layer.

Update tests in favorites.test.ts to stub retainEvent and unretainEvent, assert they are called in the right cases, and assert that Frigate errors do not cause the server function to return { ok: false }.
close_reason: retain sync stream implemented and committed

title: Add getFavoriteCount(eventId) to FavoritesStore
description:
The favorites store (src/features/camera-events/server/favorites-store.ts) tracks favorites per user, keyed (user_id, event_id). To implement last-user-unfavorites logic we need to know how many users currently have a given event favorited.

Add a getFavoriteCount(eventId: string): number method to the FavoritesStore interface and implementation. The query is:
  SELECT COUNT(*) FROM favorite_events WHERE event_id = ?

This count is read immediately after removeFavorite inside handleToggleFavorite to decide whether to call unretainEvent on Frigate. It must be called within the same synchronous SQLite operation sequence to avoid races (SQLite WAL mode serialises writes, so reading after write is safe).

Add tests in favorites-store.test.ts covering: event with zero users favorited, one user favorited, multiple users favorited, and after removal by all users.
close_reason: retain sync stream implemented and committed

title: Add retainEvent and unretainEvent to Frigate client
description:
The Frigate API exposes POST /api/events/{id}/retain and DELETE /api/events/{id}/retain to mark an event for indefinite retention or remove that mark. These are needed so favorites can sync with Frigate's retention system.

Add two methods to the Frigate client (src/features/shared/server/frigate/client.ts):
- retainEvent(eventId): POST /api/events/{eventId}/retain → FrigateResult<void>
- unretainEvent(eventId): DELETE /api/events/{eventId}/retain → FrigateResult<void>

Both are mutating calls so they must bypass the frigateCache (use frigateFetch directly with the appropriate method, not frigateGet). The mock client (mock-client.ts) must also implement stubs that return { ok: true, data: undefined } and record calls for test inspection.

Follow the existing pattern: real client checks FRIGATE_MOCK, delegates to mock-client if set. Add tests in client.test.ts and mock-client.test.ts.
close_reason: retain sync stream implemented and committed

title: Show filled red heart for favorited events
description:
The FavoriteButton renders the same outline heart regardless of whether the event is favorited. Users have no visual feedback about current favorite state. We need to thread isFavorited state from the DB through the route loaders down to the button, and show a filled red heart when favorited.

What to build:
- Update list route (camera-events.index.tsx): call getFavoritedEventIdsFn in loader alongside loadEvents, pass favoriteEventIds to CameraEventsListPage
- Update detail route (camera-events..tsx): call getIsFavoritedFn in loader alongside loadEvent, pass isFavorited to CameraEventDetailPage
- Update CameraEventsListPage props to accept favoriteEventIds:string[], pass isFavorited={favoriteEventIds.includes(event.id)} to each EventCard FavoriteButton
- Update CameraEventDetailPage props to accept isFavorited:boolean, pass to FavoriteButton
- Update FavoriteButton: add isFavorited prop, use useState for optimistic toggle on click, render Heart with fill='currentColor' and text-red-500 when favorited
- On click: optimistically flip local state, call toggleFavoriteFn, update from server response isFavorited field
- TDD: update FavoriteButton tests for filled/outline states and optimistic toggle
acceptance_criteria: Favorited events show a filled red heart. Unfavorited events show an outline heart. Clicking a favorited event's heart removes it (heart goes outline). Clicking an unfavorited event's heart adds it (heart goes filled red). Optimistic update is immediate — no waiting for server. State is sourced from DB on page load.
close_reason: FavoriteButton now accepts isFavorited prop, uses optimistic useState, aria-pressed, and filled red heart. Loaders fetch favoriteEventIds/isFavorited in parallel. Full test coverage.

title: Wire favorites store into server functions
description:
Once the favorites store exists, the server action and loaders need to use it. Currently toggleFavoriteFn discards all input and returns {ok:true}. The list and detail route loaders only fetch Frigate events — they have no concept of favorite state.

What to build:
- Update handleToggleFavorite in favorites.ts to call getFavoritesStore(), check current state, INSERT or DELETE, and return {ok:true,isFavorited:boolean}
- Update toggleFavoriteFn return type accordingly
- Add getFavoritedEventIdsFn: createServerFn GET that calls requireSession() + store.getFavoritedEventIds(userId), returns string[]
- Add getIsFavoritedFn: createServerFn GET with inputValidator for eventId, calls requireSession() + store.isFavorited(userId, eventId), returns boolean
- TDD: update existing favorites.test.ts, add tests for new server functions mocking the store
acceptance_criteria: toggleFavoriteFn returns isFavorited:true after first call, isFavorited:false after second call on same eventId. getFavoritedEventIdsFn returns array of favorited IDs for the session user. getIsFavoritedFn returns correct boolean. All three require session. All tested.
close_reason: handleToggleFavorite now accepts FavoritesStore, performs real toggle (INSERT OR IGNORE / DELETE), returns isFavorited boolean. handleGetFavoritedEventIds and handleGetIsFavorited added as pure helpers. Three createServerFn wrappers (toggleFavoriteFn, getFavoritedEventIdsFn, getIsFavoritedFn) each call requireSession() first. 13 tests passing.

title: Create favorites SQLite store
description:
The toggle server action has no persistence — it validates and discards. We need a favorites-store.ts following the push-store.ts singleton pattern to persist favorite_events to SQLite. This is the data layer that all other favorites work depends on.

What to build:
- src/features/camera-events/server/favorites-store.ts
- Table: favorite_events (user_id TEXT, event_id TEXT, created_at INTEGER, PRIMARY KEY (user_id, event_id))
- Open DB in WAL mode via openSqlite('data/camera-events.db')
- Pre-compile statements in a stmts object (same pattern as push-store.ts)
- Methods: addFavorite(userId, eventId), removeFavorite(userId, eventId), isFavorited(userId, eventId): boolean, getFavoritedEventIds(userId): string[]
- Lazy singleton via getFavoritesStore()
- Schema migration runs on first call (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS)
- TDD: write tests first covering all methods, including edge cases (double-add, remove non-existent)
acceptance_criteria: getFavoritesStore() returns a store with addFavorite, removeFavorite, isFavorited, getFavoritedEventIds. Table is created on first call. isFavorited returns false for unknown IDs. addFavorite is idempotent (double-add does not error). removeFavorite on non-existent entry does not error. All methods tested.
close_reason: favorites-store.ts created following push-store.ts pattern. CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS favorite_events (user_id, event_id, created_at, PRIMARY KEY). INSERT OR IGNORE for idempotent add, DELETE for remove, prepared statements, WAL mode, lazy singleton. 14 tests passing.

title: Add empty toggleFavorite server action for camera events
description: The favorite button on camera events pages needs a server action to call when clicked. The action should be a createServerFn handler that calls requireSession() and accepts an event ID, but performs no side effects for now. It exists as a placeholder until the favorites persistence layer is designed.
acceptance_criteria: toggleFavorite server action exists, calls requireSession() as first operation, accepts a valid event ID, and returns without error. No persistence logic yet.
close_reason: Pure handleToggleFavorite function + toggleFavoriteFn server action created in src/features/camera-events/server/favorites.ts. Calls requireSession(), validates event ID with isValidEventId(), returns { ok: true } as placeholder. 4 tests passing.

title: Add favorite heart icon button to camera events pages
description: Users have no way to mark individual camera events as favorites. A Lucide heart icon button should appear after the event title on both the event list and event detail pages. Clicking it should call a toggleFavorite server action. The button must be accessible and keyboard-navigable.
acceptance_criteria: Heart icon button renders after event title on /camera-events list and /camera-events/:id detail pages. Button is visually distinct and accessible. Clicking the button invokes the toggleFavorite server action.
close_reason: FavoriteButton component added (Heart icon, aria-label, calls toggleFavoriteFn on click). Placed after the h1 title on the detail page and as an absolute-positioned sibling to MediaCard in the list page EventCard (avoids nested interactive elements). 3 tests added.

title: Update CLAUDE.md and project docs to reflect Node.js/pnpm stack
description:
After the Node.js/pnpm migration is complete, all project documentation that references Bun must be updated. This is a cleanup/polish task to be done last.

## Files to update

CLAUDE.md (root):
- Project Overview: change 'deployed via Bun' to 'deployed via Node.js'
- Package manager section: change all references from bun to pnpm
- Development Commands: change bun run dev/build/test/etc to pnpm run ...
- SQLite section: remove the runtime-portable driver explanation, simplify to 'uses better-sqlite3'
- Remove the bun:sqlite, isBunRuntime, bun-branch/bun-runtime test file naming conventions
- Remove *.bun-runtime.test.ts and *.bun-branch.test.ts from the special test variants section
- Update deployment section to reference Node.js runtime
- Remove any ENV var notes specific to Bun

docs/ (if present):
- docs/claude/deployment.md: update runtime notes
- docs/claude/testing.md: remove bun-specific test patterns
- docs/claude/backend.md: update SQLite driver architecture description

## What NOT to change
- Keep the test file naming convention docs (*.driver-contract.test.ts is still valid)
- Keep the SqliteDatabase interface documentation (still relevant)
- Do not rewrite docs that are still accurate

## Acceptance criteria
- No remaining references to 'bun run' in CLAUDE.md or docs/
- SQLite section accurately describes the simplified better-sqlite3-only driver
- Development commands section shows pnpm commands throughout
- A fresh developer following CLAUDE.md will not be confused about which runtime is expected
